title: podman: split env variables in env and overrides
body:
There are three different priorities for applying env variables:

1) environment/config file environment variables
2) image's config
3) user overrides (--env)

The third kind are known to the client, while the default config and image's
config is handled by the backend.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I believe this should replace https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/6100

But we still have issues with --http-proxy and --env-host.

Where should these defaults come from.  In a different conversation, it was suggested that --http-proxy should come from the server, currently it is coming from the client.

I think if we want this to be used on the server, we would also need to handle --env-host on the server side as well, or we would need to send over a different set of enviornment variables from client to server.

The question is whether or not sending the environment from a MAC, Windows, Linux box over the network to a server makes sense, or would the user expect the environment from the server to be handled?

I still see potential issues with `--env-file` here - it will override image environment like this, which I think is incorrect. Though maybe we just provide paths to the files in the SpecGen? That may well be what Docker does (I don't think they parse it in the frontend)
I updated this PR to add another patch to move http-proxy to server side.
I doubt we actually have tests for proper parsing order in environment variables. All the `podman run` tests passed without any of this being added.
